name: Watermelon Rind Pickle
recipeInstructions:
1. Pare off the outer green from watermelon rind and cut into 1-inch squares.
2. Place in a large bowl and pour over them the salt and water mixture. Let soak for three days.
3. Drain and let stand in fresh water for one hour.
4. Make a syrup of sugar, vinegar, water, and spices: combine 6.5 cups brown sugar, 2 cups vinegar, 1 cup water, 1 tablespoon whole cloves, and 2 cinnamon sticks in a saucepan. Heat until the sugar dissolves completely.
5. Cut the lemons (rind and all) paper thin.
6. Place the melon and lemon slices into the hot syrup. Stir to coat evenly.
7. Bring the mixture back to a boil over medium heat, then simmer for 10 minutes.
8. Pack the watermelon into sterilized jars, leaving some headspace at the top.
9. Seal the jars tightly.
From South Union, Ky. In The Shaker Cook Book: Not by Bread Alone by Caroline B. Piercy. New York: Crown Publishers, Inc., 1953. Pg. 183. Library of Congress Catalog Card Number 53-5248.
